element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Martial arts initiate in Tokyo dojo
Gateway to Second World and combat encounters
Sequential boss/enemy encounters through Second World
Primary antagonist requiring defeat to rescue Nezu

element_introduction_order.element: Beast (Sett-Ra minion returning to real world)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Secondary boss encounter after dimensional return
Optional advanced combat technique via button sequence

typical_relief_triggers: Return to real world narrative beat
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Tournament/arcade fighting game rhythm: discrete match intervals with story beats between encounters; rapid-paced sprite-based combat with fast game speed noted by reviewers

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden blood code activated via button sequence (similar to Mortal Kombat conventions)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-oriented with Leotsu mentor figure in dojo setting; accessible narrative introduction
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive enemy encounters in Second World with increasing opponent difficulty
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sett-Ra boss encounter followed by Beast rematch; climactic narrative conclusion

notable_level_patterns.description: Story progresses from real world (Tokyo dojo) to Second World (alternate dimension) and returns to real world for final confrontation
notable_level_patterns.example_level: North Gate/South Gate stage (accessible by default in Genesis version)

notable_level_patterns.description: Five additional stages beyond SNES version including The Lab, The Wasteland, and Yasko Mines; provides longer tournament progression
Genesis version features five additional playable characters (Auroch, Colonel, Diesel, Leotsu, Nezu) compared to SNES; supports tournament/arcade ladder structure
